source_description: Inside a kitchen with light blue cabinets, Monica sits at a wooden table wearing an orange cardigan over a patterned top. Chandler, in a green short-sleeved button-down over a white shirt, sits opposite her. Audience laughter is audible. "Room changes," the text reads.
Chandler maintains eye contact with Monica as she keeps her hands clasped on the table.
Chandler gestures with both hands and says, "Yeah, I mean, we're going to have to move around some furniture to make room for my chair." Monica watches him, her shoulders still.
Chandler gestures with both hands and says, "Yeah, I mean, we're going to have to move around some furniture to make room for my chair." Monica watches him, her shoulders still.
Chandler leans forward and kisses Monica. He then stands up and walks toward the refrigerator in the background. Monica turns her head to follow his movement. Audience laughter continues.
Chandler leans forward and kisses Monica. He then stands up and walks toward the refrigerator in the background. Monica turns her head to follow his movement. Audience laughter continues.
Monica remains seated at the table, her gaze fixed on Chandler. "You're going to bring the barca lounger over here?" she asks.
Chandler stands near the refrigerator, facing Monica. He asks, "Is that a problem?"
Monica lowers her gaze to the table, her hands remaining clasped.
Monica gestures with her hands as she says, "it's just that they're a set and they should probably stay together." Chandler stands with his hands at his sides, looking toward her.

Monica raises one hand while Chandler keeps his gaze on her.
Monica raises her index finger and looks toward the ceiling, saying, "See, now you're taking them away from their home."

Chandler spreads his arms wide and says, "Okay, I get it. So I get nothing. Nothing here is mine." His volume is louder than his previous line.

Chandler continues, his pace faster than before: "Everything is yours. I'll get up in the morning, put on your clothes and head off to-" He gestures toward his own chest and then outward.
Chandler continues, his pace faster than before: "Everything is yours. I'll get up in the morning, put on your clothes and head off to-" He gestures toward his own chest and then outward.
Monica stands up quickly from the table, facing Chandler.
Monica says, "yeah, yeah, you know yep. That's it. That's it." She moves her hands in a small circular motion.
Monica walks toward Chandler, reducing the distance between them. "Everything will be mine. Nothing will be yours," she says, her palms open.
